edges.description: Occlusive cerebrovascular disease produces recurrent transient ischemic attacks and strokes.
The same vasculopathy produces the characteristic generalized livedo skin findings.
Recurrent cerebral ischemic injury manifests clinically as ischemic stroke.
The cutaneous vascular phenotype is expressed clinically as livedo reticularis.

nodes.description: Recurrent occlusive cerebrovascular events produce stroke, cerebral atrophy, and long-term neurologic impairment.

evidence.reference_title: Clinical, neurovascular and neuropathological features in Sneddon's syndrome.
evidence.snippet: The patients had repeated ischemic cerebral episodes, livedo reticularis and thrombocytopenia. CT and MRI showed strokes and cerebral atrophy.

nodes.description: Generalized livedo reticularis or livedo racemosa is the characteristic dermatologic expression of the underlying vasculopathy.

evidence.reference_title: Clinical, neurovascular and neuropathological features in Sneddon's syndrome.
evidence.snippet: Sneddon's syndrome (SS) is characterized by ischemic cerebrovascular episodes and livedo reticularis.

nodes.description: Recurrent ischemic cerebral events are a central neurologic manifestation of Sneddon syndrome.

evidence.reference_title: Clinical, neurovascular and neuropathological features in Sneddon's syndrome.
evidence.snippet: The patients had repeated ischemic cerebral episodes, livedo reticularis and thrombocytopenia. CT and MRI showed strokes and cerebral atrophy.

nodes.description: Persistent livedo reticularis or livedo racemosa is a defining skin finding in Sneddon syndrome.

evidence.reference_title: Clinical, neurovascular and neuropathological features in Sneddon's syndrome.
evidence.snippet: Sneddon's syndrome (SS) is characterized by ischemic cerebrovascular episodes and livedo reticularis.

nodes.description: Sneddon syndrome is understood as an occlusive vasculopathy or thrombotic microangiopathy affecting small- and medium-sized vessels.

evidence.reference_title: Combination therapy for Sneddon syndrome to reduce the incidence of cerebrovascular complications
evidence.snippet: Sneddon syndrome is an occlusive vasculopathy that presents clinically with generalized livedo racemosa on the skin and transient ischemic attacks, strokes, and cognitive or motor deficits in the central nervous system.
